seoTitle: Phillip Bay vs Phoenix Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Phillip Bay and Phoenix Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Phillip Bay vs Phoenix Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Phillip Bay and Phoenix Park.
heading: Phillip Bay vs Phoenix Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Phillip Bay, New South Wales and Phoenix Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Phoenix Park has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Phillip Bay (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Phillip Bay or Phoenix Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Phillip Bay has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Phoenix Park scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Phillip Bay scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Phoenix Park scores 47/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Phillip Bay or Phoenix Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Phoenix Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
